nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; lagoon and seaward reefs at 1–15 m depth. Obligate symbiont of host anemones, primarily Heteractis magnifica and Stichodactyla gigantea, occasionally Heteractis crispa.

diet.notes: Accepts most prepared foods. A varied diet of high-quality marine pellets, flakes, and enriched frozen foods (mysis, brine) is generally advised to support coloration and immune function.

summary: A small, peaceful anemonefish from the western Pacific. Generally considered one of the most beginner-friendly marine fish; widely captive-bred in a range of designer color morphs.
careNotes:
One entry covers Amphiprion percula and all its captive-bred designer morphs. Trade names like 'Semi Picasso', 'Picasso', 'Extreme Picasso', 'Platinum', and 'Onyx' refer to selectively bred color/pattern variants graded by the amount and shape of white markings — the 'Semi Picasso' grade has irregular white patches outlined in black but less white than a standard Picasso (incomplete bands rather than full ones), the standard 'Picasso' grade has more developed irregular white patches, and the 'Extreme Picasso' grade is the most heavily white-marked and is described by suppliers as having unique markings on every individual. The 'Platinum Percula' is an ORA-developed designer morph derived through years of selective breeding from the Picasso line; it has a near-completely white body that retains essentially none of the species' typical orange-and-black striping, and as the fish matures it develops black fins and an orange-tinged face that contrast sharply with the white body. These morphs do not occur in the wild and share the husbandry of the wild form.

Generally considered beginner-friendly. Best kept singly or as a mated pair; have been known to be aggressive toward other clownfish species and toward unfamiliar conspecifics, so introduce pairs as juveniles or buy a bonded pair. Does not require a host anemone in captivity, though it will host one if a compatible species (typically Heteractis magnifica, Stichodactyla gigantea, or sometimes carpet/bubble-tip anemones) is offered.

Like all clownfish, Amphiprion percula is a protandrous sequential hermaphrodite — every fish is born male, and the largest fish in a group becomes the dominant female. Within a group there is a strict size-based hierarchy of one breeding female, one breeding male, and several non-breeding subordinates.

Source spread on the upper temperature was modest: aquaticsunlimited gave 22–26°C (72–78°F) for captive husbandry, while FishBase wild-temperature data centres around 25.5–28.4°C. The 24–27°C range written here averages the two.
breedingNotes: Has been bred extensively in captivity and is one of the easiest marine fish to breed. Spawns demersally — pairs lay adhesive eggs on a hard surface near their host anemone, and the male tends the clutch. Larvae are pelagic and require rotifer-grade feeds initially, then enriched Artemia.

primary.name: aquaticsunlimited.com
primary.notes: Species page for the Extreme Picasso variant; provided the captive husbandry parameters (size, temperature, pH, salinity, tank size, diet, temperament, care level).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed taxonomy (Pomacentridae), wild distribution (Queensland, Melanesia, GBR, New Guinea, Solomon Islands, Vanuatu), wild temperature range (25.5–28.4°C, mean 27.3°C), depth (1–15 m), and host-anemone associations.

additional.name: Wikipedia (Orange clownfish)
additional.notes: Confirmed maximum length 11 cm (avg 8 cm), breeding-female tenure ~12 years, protandrous sex-change biology, and pair-bonded social structure with size-based hierarchy.

additional.name: LiveAquaria
additional.notes: Manifest primary source; the collections page is a catalog index rather than a species page and did not list per-species husbandry details. Cross-verification fell to FishBase + Wikipedia.

additional.name: saltwateraquarium.com
additional.notes: Merge source for the 'Platinum Percula Clownfish' designer-morph trade name. Confirmed species is Amphiprion percula. Confirmed ORA developed the Platinum Percula through selective breeding from their Picasso Clownfish line, that mature specimens have a near-totally white body with no remaining orange-and-black striping, and that they develop black fins and an orange face as they mature. No husbandry parameters provided; trade name was already present in alsoKnownAs and is now described in careNotes.

additional.name: Sea & Reef Aquaculture + LiveAquaria (Semi Picasso Clownfish)
additional.notes: Combined merge sources for the 'Semi Picasso Clownfish' trade name. LiveAquaria's Semi Picasso Clownfish product page (https://www.liveaquaria.com/product/3334/) confirms the variant is Amphiprion percula and describes it as 'orange with irregularly shaped white patches outlined in black' with less white than the standard Picasso grade; captive-bred. Sea & Reef Aquaculture's Picasso clownfish page (https://www.seaandreef.com/marine-ornamental-fish/clownfish/picasso) confirms Picasso grades of A. percula are graded by 'the amount and shape of the white markings' and lists Picasso, Premium Picasso, and Picasso Misbar — 'Semi Picasso' is a retailer designation between Misbar and standard Picasso rather than a Sea & Reef-named grade.

fish.breedingNotes: Pairs spawn readily in mature reef tanks. Rearing larvae is moderate-difficulty — they require rotifers as a first food before transitioning to enriched Artemia.
